# AI Warning: This project is generated by Gemini 3

I have zero knowledge of Python. Therefore, for any modifications within this repository, asking an AI might be faster than waiting for me to fix the issues myself.

# Karaoke Lyric Generator

This software was created because I wanted to make karaoke videos and simultaneously test the workflow of AI-assisted programming. Currently, it only supports word-by-word lyric formats (similar to QQ Music), such as:

> [00:11.975]無[00:12.127]我[00:12.289]夢[00:12.478]中[00:12.815]で[00:13.215]踊[00:13.563]る[00:13.806] [00:13.806]Day & [00:14.600]Night[00:15.015]
[00:15.015]ぎ[00:15.176]こ[00:15.317]ち[00:15.547]な[00:15.694]い[00:15.813]ス[00:15.963]テ[00:16.067]ッ[00:16.170]プ[00:16.464]刻[00:16.821]ん[00:17.178]だ[00:17.561]

I have only tested this with Chinese, English, and Japanese songs. The code is specifically optimized for mixed Chinese and English content.

### Current Features

- Lyric fade-in and fade-out.
    
- Countdown timer before the first line starts after a long interlude.
    
- Automatic splitting of long lyric lines.
    
- Support for displaying backing vocals at the top.
    

### Currently Not Supported

- Bilingual subtitles.
    
- Subtitles with pronunciation/phonetics (e.g., Furigana or Pinyin).
    

---

# How to Generate Subtitles

1. Download `kolyric.py` from the repository and place it in a folder.
    
2. Open this folder with any IDE, or simply use Notepad to edit the `.py` file.
    
3. Download your lyric file and place it in the same folder as `kolyric.py`.
    
4. Modify the settings section at the top of the script according to your needs. I have labeled each setting with comments.
    
5. Run the `.py` program using an IDE or terminal. After execution, you will find the generated `.ass` file in the same directory.
    

> **How to find the font name?**
> 
> In Windows Settings, search for "font", click on the font you want, and look for the "Full name" in the first line of the "Metadata" section.

---

# How to Use Subtitles

- **External Subtitles**: Simply rename the subtitle file (the part before `.ass`) to match the name of your song MV file.
    

> **Note**: Some song MVs include plot interludes or different arrangements, which may cause the subtitles to go out of sync.

## FFmpeg Video Composition Guide

My own workflow uses instruction 3.

The following commands should be executed in a terminal or command prompt. Ensure **FFmpeg** is installed and replace filenames/parameters as needed.

>**Note**: For some songs, the instrumental and vocal versions may have different options. You can use editing software to view the waveforms of these two audio files and align them.
### Command 1: Generate Green Screen Subtitle Video

Use this if you need subtitles as a standalone asset for video editing software:


```Bash
ffmpeg -f lavfi -i color=size=1920x1080:rate=60:color=green -t [Duration_of_lyrics] -vf "ass=output.ass" -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p green_screen.mp4
```

- **Modifiable Items**:
    
    - `size=1920x1080`: Resolution (must match your subtitle settings).
        
    - `rate=60`: Frame rate (can be changed to 30 if high FPS is not needed).
        
    - `color=green`: Background color (change to blue if your subtitles are green).
        

### Command 2: Combine MV + 70% Instrumental + 30% Vocal + Subtitles (Mute Original)

Use this for a one-step final render. It mutes the original MV, mixes your instrumental and vocal tracks, and hard-codes the subtitles:


```Bash
ffmpeg -i input_mv.mp4 -i instrumental.mp3 -i vocal.mp3 -filter_complex "[1:a]volume=0.7[inst];[2:a]volume=0.3[voc];[inst][voc]amix=inputs=2:duration=longest[aout]" -map 0:v:0 -map "[aout]" -vf "ass=output.ass" -c:v libx264 -c:a aac -shortest final_mixed_mv.mp4
```

- **Modifiable Items**:
    
    - `input_mv.mp4`: Your original video file.
        
    - `instrumental.mp3`: Your background track.
        
    - `vocal.mp3`: Your extracted vocal track.
        
    - `[1:a]volume=0.7`: Instrumental volume ratio. Next volume settings is vocal ratio.
        

### ⭕（Recommend）Command 3: Generate Clean Plate for VFX (Mute Original + Hardcode Subtitles)


```Bash
ffmpeg -i input_mv.mp4 -vf "ass=output.ass" -c:v libx264 -an final_vfx_mv.mp4
```

- **Modifiable Items**:
    
    - `input_mv.mp4`: Your original video file.
        
    - `-an`: This parameter forces the audio to be muted.
